title: phragmites australis var. humilis
intro: Phragmites australis var. humilis, commonly known as dwarf or low-growing reed grass, is a type of plant that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a perennial grass that is native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia, and is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the body. The herbal preparations of Phragmites australis var. humilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
